contract.independentOracle: A direct Factorio physical meter independently observes the loaded fixture before accepting a result.
contract.resetModel: Reload the paired source and destination golden saves at the batch boundary; never repair a consumed fixture.

labs.title: Belts and transport lines
labs.purpose: Visualize lane-side fidelity, native stacks, corners, splitters, undergrounds, and known loss replays.

labs.title: Paired-reads census
labs.purpose: Ground the per-entity paired-read census meter: stall budget and serialize-vs-physical commensurability at the export gate.

labs.title: Engine version repin
labs.purpose: Re-certify engine-sensitive runtime shapes and behavioral rungs after a Factorio version change.

labs.title: Fluid systems
labs.purpose: Inspect fluidbox ownership, frozen reads, pumps, plasma, and exact fluid accounting.

labs.title: Validation gate drift
labs.purpose: Exercise frozen-gate timing and detect source or destination state that escapes the exact verdict.

labs.title: Hidden state semantics
labs.purpose: Probe state that is structurally present but easy for serializers or validators to miss.

labs.title: Destination hold completeness
labs.purpose: Demonstrate which entities and operations must remain frozen until transfer validation completes.

labs.title: Inserter held stacks
labs.purpose: Visualize held-item reachability, quality, filters, and busy-state round trips.

labs.title: Mid-craft machines
labs.purpose: Inspect recipe progress, ingredient embodiment, and partially completed machine state.

labs.title: No-tick synchronization
labs.purpose: Prove restoration and validation behavior while the game is paused and no normal tick advances.

labs.title: Operational tails
labs.purpose: Exercise cleanup, retry, timeout, and transaction-tail behavior around completed operations.

labs.title: Specialized inventories
labs.purpose: Map reachable inventories and fluids owned by specialized entity handlers.

labs.title: Serialized state dimensions
labs.purpose: Audit energy, temperature, burner, bonus progress, equipment, and other player-visible state dimensions.

fixtures.name: Specialized fluid reachability
fixtures.purpose: Recertify platform-reachable specialized fluid state from prototype and live entity controls.
fixtures.note: Revision 2: the drill was recreated after an asteroid loss and all platform entities are now destructible=false (asteroid-hardening).
fixtures.invariant: Space-platform pressure/gravity are zero and the baked electric mining drill has no live recoverable fluidbox; every fixture entity is destructible=false.

fixtures.name: Adversarial nested inventory + quality
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a steel-chest holding a power-armor-mk2 whose equipment grid carries a legendary battery and uncommon shield, beside an uncommon-quality iron-gear-wheel recipe.
fixtures.invariant: The chest holds a power-armor-mk2 with a legendary battery-mk2-equipment (energy 5000000) and an uncommon energy-shield-mk2-equipment (shield 97, max 195); the assembling-machine-2 runs the iron-gear-wheel recipe at uncommon quality.

testCard.law: Nested inventories survive exactly: a chest-held power-armor-mk2 keeps its equipment grid (battery energy, shield value, qualities) and the machine keeps its quality recipe.
testCard.build: Migrated 2026-07-18 from the legacy y=0 zone onto the test-foundation pad at (8,-20) via surface.clone_area (seed-prep migrate_omnibus_zone; exact-state move, originals destroyed).
testCard.action: /test-run: reset the compare area, selection-lab copy of the pad build area, paste at +14,0, physical audit of both halves.
testCard.expected: Audit reports equal entities/items; the pasted armor's grid carries the same battery energy, shield value, and equipment qualities.
testCard.forbidden: An emptied or flattened equipment grid; lost item quality; a dropped quality recipe.

fixtures.note: VAULT ROLE (owner, 2026-07-19): the steel-chest at (12.5,-16.5) is the item-coverage vault — item-data classes needing serialization checking live here (blueprint w/ layout+label, blueprint-book w/ nested print, filtered decon planner, partial-durability repair pack, partial magazine, stable spoilage x10). Mid-spoil items are spawned ON DEMAND only (spoil timers tick even frozen). Meter fields unchanged (armor-specific).

fixtures.name: Accumulator drain isolation
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a charged accumulator that is the sole electric entity, documenting the drain law.
fixtures.note: Drain law: active=false does NOT stop accumulator discharge; a ~5kW deactivated machine measurably bled a deactivated accumulator, so the fixture carries no other electric entity.
fixtures.invariant: The accumulator holds energy 3000000 and is the sole electric entity on the platform; on a generation-free platform any consumer would bleed it, so it is kept alone (drain law).

fixtures.name: 1359-entity transfer workhorse
fixtures.purpose: Provide the large live-drift transfer workhorse whose structure is fixed but whose machine item counts drift by design.
fixtures.note: Structure-only fingerprint. Machines are live: measured iron-plate drifted 1688 -> 1680 over hours; only entity count is stable.
fixtures.invariant: The platform carries exactly 1359 entities; its machines are deliberately LIVE so item counts drift over time and must never be fingerprinted.

fixtures.name: Held-item capacity on a less-researched destination
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a frozen legendary bulk-inserter whose hand is seated with 8 railgun-ammo under a baked >=11 player-force bulk-inserter capacity bonus - the adversarial state for dest-force research replication on import.
fixtures.note: Script-built by tests/lab-gallery/seed-prep-ops.lua (build_inserter_held) onto the stamped inserter-held-capacity cell at (92,8): raise the player force bulk_inserter_capacity_bonus to >=11 (raise-only, logged), create a legendary bulk-inserter at (98.5,13.5), seat 8 LEGENDARY railgun-ammo via plain held_stack.set_stack (no active toggle; inserter-lab B6), then freeze (active=false, destructible=false). Re-baked 2026-07-18: the original bake seated a NORMAL-quality stack and self-certified via the entity-quality meter (B7 run 1b caught it via the independent stack-quality read); the stack is now legendary and both meters read the STACK. forceBulkBonus is a placeholder floor; the live bake pins the exact baked value. The ADVERSARIAL destination state is baked too (2026-07-18): the golden dest descends from the long-lived local host-2 lineage whose player force had bulk 11 / stack 3 (B7 run 1 caught it — the fixture could not discriminate the Pitfall #29 fix), so both inserter bonuses were zeroed at bake time and the dest artifact re-pinned; the runner still ASSERTS 0 at load, never forces.
fixtures.invariant: A legendary bulk-inserter holds 8 railgun-ammo in its hand while inactive and indestructible; the player force bulk_inserter_capacity_bonus is >= 11 so the hand seats in full and survives save/load.

testCard.law: Held items seat IN FULL on a less-researched destination: export captures the source-force inserter bonuses, import replicates them raise-only, and the strict gate counts the complete state (Pitfalls #28 count-a-complete-state / #29 dest-force research).
testCard.build: lab-omnibus-state-v1 inserter-held-capacity cell (34,-128): a legendary bulk-inserter at (98.5,13.5) holds 8 railgun-ammo via plain held_stack.set_stack (no active toggle); the player force bulk_inserter_capacity_bonus is baked >= 11; the inserter is frozen (active=false, destructible=false).
testCard.action: Production /transfer-platform of the omnibus platform to a destination whose bulk_inserter_capacity_bonus is 0 (asserted, never forced).
testCard.expected: A forceDataMismatches warning fires; the destination bonus is raised, never lowered; the hand seats 8/8 by physical count; the strict gate reports SUCCESS.
testCard.forbidden: An under-seated hand; a gate pass over a short hand; any destination bonus lowered; the baked bonus not surviving save/load.

fixtures.name: No-tick synchronous held-item pair
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a frozen mid-craft assembling-machine-1 beside an inactive empty-handed inserter - the construct-free target proving the synchronous held-item pass advances no tick.
fixtures.note: Script-built by tests/lab-gallery/seed-prep-ops.lua (build_no_tick_pair) onto the stamped no-tick-sync-frozen-pair cell at (8,22): an assembling-machine-1 at (13.5,27.5) set to iron-gear-wheel with 4 iron-plate in crafter_input and crafting_progress write-asserted to 0.42, then frozen; a plain west-facing inserter at (16.5,27.5), empty-handed, frozen. progress absorbs a crafting-progress ULP on save/load.
fixtures.invariant: The assembling-machine-1 is inactive with crafting_progress ~0.42, iron-gear-wheel set, and 4 iron-plate embodied; the neighboring inserter is inactive with an empty hand; every fixture entity is destructible=false.

testCard.law: The synchronous held-item pass leaves game.tick, crafting_progress, and machine inputs UNCHANGED while it seats the inserter hand, reproduced from a save-loaded world (no-tick-sync-lab).
testCard.build: lab-omnibus-state-v1 no-tick-sync-frozen-pair cell (34,-114): an inactive assembling-machine-1 at (13.5,27.5) frozen mid-craft (iron-gear-wheel, 4 iron-plate embodied, crafting_progress 0.42 write-asserted) beside an inactive west-facing empty-handed inserter at (16.5,27.5); both destructible=false.
testCard.action: Run the no_tick_sync_selftest remote in measure-baked-fixture (construct-free) mode against this pair under tick-pause - the strongest form.
testCard.expected: game.tick unchanged; crafting_progress exactly 0.42 after the pass; the hand seats in full.
testCard.forbidden: Any tick advance during the pass; crafting_progress drift; the selftest constructing anything on the golden world.

fixtures.name: Beacon crafting_speed same-execution propagation
fixtures.purpose: Preserve an ACTIVE empty-module beacon beside a frozen recipe-set crafter - the baked baseline for the engine-repin B8 pause-free rung (module population must raise crafting_speed in the SAME Lua execution, no ticks, no power).
fixtures.note: Script-built by tests/lab-gallery/seed-prep-ops.lua (build_repin_beacon) onto the stamped repin-beacon-speed cell at (36,22): an active beacon with an EMPTY beacon_modules inventory at (40.5,26.5) and a frozen (active=false) assembling-machine-2 with iron-gear-wheel set at (44.5,26.5), both indestructible. The batch rung inserts 2 speed modules, reads crafting_speed same-execution, clears, and confirms the baseline - single-use per golden load; reload restores the empty-module baseline. NAMING: this is engine-repin B8, DISTINCT from no-tick-sync B8 - always cite lab-prefixed.
fixtures.invariant: The beacon is active with an empty module inventory; the machine reads crafting_speed 0.75 (unboosted assembling-machine-2), is inactive with iron-gear-wheel set; both are indestructible.

testCard.law: Populating a beacon's module inventory raises nearby crafting_speed in the SAME Lua execution - no tick, no power (engine-repin B8; the mechanism behind the two-pass beacon-first inventory restore, CLAUDE.md Import Phase Ordering).
testCard.build: repin-beacon-speed cell at (36,22): active empty-module beacon at (40.5,26.5), frozen assembling-machine-2 (iron-gear-wheel, crafting_speed 0.75) at (44.5,26.5), both indestructible.
testCard.action: Batch rung: insert 2 speed modules into beacon_modules, read machine.crafting_speed in the same execution, clear the modules, confirm the baseline returns.
testCard.expected: Speed rises immediately upon module insertion (same execution, machine still inactive and unpowered) and returns to 0.75 when the modules clear.
testCard.forbidden: Speed requiring elapsed ticks or power to update; a baseline that is not 0.75; modules left behind after the rung.

fixtures.name: Hold buffer pair: spoilage
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a live/held pair of generation-free mini-platforms each holding one spoilable seeded to ~0.95 spoil - the baked baseline for the destination-hold spoilage drift law (held drift <= live-control drift over a ~5s window).
fixtures.note: Script-built by seed-prep-ops.lua (build_hold_spoil): each platform carries a hub and one indestructible steel-chest with 1x of the longest-spoil-time spoilable seeded to spoil_percent 0.95 (RE-SEEDED on every bake - spoil_tick is engine-global and advances every loaded session). Platforms baked PAUSED for stability; the batch unpauses both before staging so the hold's own semantics govern the held side. spoilSeeded fingerprints are booleans (0.5 < spoil < 1) because the raw float drifts within every session.
fixtures.invariant: Both chests hold exactly one spoilable with spoil_percent in (0.5, 1); both platforms are paused; hold records are staged only at runtime via the production remote.

testCard.law: A destination hold preserves spoilage state no worse than live drift: held spoil drift <= paired live-control drift, and nothing leaves the platform (PR0A lineage; test-contract boundaries - spoilage transfer is the WHOLE contract).
testCard.build: lab-hold-spoil-{live,held}-v1: generation-free mini-platforms, one indestructible steel-chest each holding 1x spoilable at spoil ~0.95, baked paused.
testCard.action: Batch: fingerprint-verify, unpause both, stage a hold on the HELD platform via the production destination_hold_json remote, wait ~5 seconds, measure spoil drift on both sides by independent physical read, discard the hold.
testCard.expected: Held drift <= live drift; the chest and its stack survive on the held platform; discard leaves zero destination_holds; zero transient plugin state at exit.
testCard.forbidden: Hold-side exclusive loss; a window stretched beyond ~5s or extra measurands (contract boundaries); leaked hold records; the runner repairing any fixture mid-batch.

fixtures.name: Hold buffer pair: damage
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a live/held pair whose DESTRUCTIBLE chest targets sit beside neutral asteroid specimens - the baked baseline for the zero-hold-attributable-damage law.
fixtures.note: Script-built by seed-prep-ops.lua (build_hold_damage): a steel-chest target that STAYS DESTRUCTIBLE (owner adjudication: damage is the measurand - safe in the golden save because zips do not age and the platforms are baked paused) beside a neutral asteroid specimen at 2 tiles.
fixtures.invariant: Both chests are destructible at full health beside a valid neutral asteroid; both platforms paused.

testCard.law: A destination hold causes zero hold-attributable platform damage: held-side target health never decreases below the live control's behavior, and the asteroid stays contained (or terminates identically to live).
testCard.build: lab-hold-damage-{live,held}-v1: destructible steel-chest at full health + neutral asteroid specimen 2 tiles away, baked paused.
testCard.action: Batch: fingerprint-verify, unpause both, stage a hold on the HELD platform via the production remote, wait ~5 seconds, measure target health + asteroid state on both sides, discard the hold.
testCard.expected: Zero damage delta attributable to the hold (held target health non-decreasing); held drift <= live drift; discard leaves zero destination_holds.
testCard.forbidden: Hold-side exclusive damage; an indestructible target (it would blind the measurand); leaked hold records; mid-batch fixture repair.

fixtures.name: Hold buffer pair: cargo pod
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a live/held pair with a FULL hub and a loaded cargo-pod - the baked baseline for the pod-absorption overflow law (staging leaves the held platform pod-free with all cargo retained on the platform).
fixtures.note: Script-built by seed-prep-ops.lua (build_hold_pod): every hub_main slot filled with 100 iron-plate (a FULL hub forces the pod-absorption overflow branch), baked paused with NO pod. An in-flight pod is TRANSIENT state that cannot be baked - cargo_pod_state is READ-ONLY (measured v15: 'LuaEntity::cargo_pod_state is read only') and a baked pod decays to 'ascending', which the production hold force-finishes (cargo counted as sent, measured v15) - so the pod joins the runtime-staging deviation: the batch creates it loaded with 100 copper-plate in the SAME Lua execution as the stage call (the PR0A-proven recipe).
fixtures.invariant: Both platforms carry a hub with every slot iron-filled and ZERO baked pods; both platforms paused; the in-flight pod + hold record are runtime-staged only.

testCard.law: Staging a destination hold absorbs in-flight cargo pods without loss: the held platform becomes pod-free and every unit of the pod's cargo remains ON the platform (hub or ground) - nothing leaves.
testCard.build: lab-hold-pod-{live,held}-v1: FULL iron-filled hub, baked paused, zero baked pods (an in-flight pod is unbakeable transient state - see note).
testCard.action: Batch: fingerprint-verify, then in ONE Lua execution create a copper-loaded pod on each side and stage the hold on the HELD platform via the production remote; wait ~5 seconds; measure pod count + total platform copper (hub + ground + pod) on both sides; discard the hold.
testCard.expected: Held platform pod-free after stage with >= 100 copper retained on-platform; live control keeps its pod; discard leaves zero destination_holds.
testCard.forbidden: Any copper leaving the held platform; a hold leaving the pod behind un-absorbed while claiming staged; leaked hold records; mid-batch fixture repair (the runtime pod+hold staging is the ONE sanctioned exception, per the card note).

fixtures.name: Combined belt omnibus (steady-state, hand-built)
fixtures.purpose: One pad combining sideloads, 3 splitters, 3 underground pairs, and 2 filtered output loaders draining into infinity chests pinned at 100 - the steady-state fixture class exemplar: fully live, item count constant by saturation physics.
fixtures.owningRunnerWaiver: Hand-built live by the owner 2026-07-19 (construct-once doctrine). No build script exists by design; the state itself is the artifact, banked in the source-of-truth checkpoint.
fixtures.invariant: CONSERVATIVE closed circuit (owner removed the infinity chests 2026-07-19: self-healing masks loss): 380 items stationary across 35 turbo belts / 3 splitters / 6 underground ends / 2 filtered loaders; every stack one item; corner inside lanes carry the over-pack class (measured 18-19 lanes, pinned as presence). Covers the retired belt-corner-recovery invariant (over-pack, 18 lanes vs its 4) and the conservation-honesty property.

testCard.law: A saturated closed belt circuit is STEADY-STATE: item count constant while fully live (no freeze needed - belts consume nothing; saturation is a fixed point).
testCard.action: /test-run copies the live circuit and audits the copy in the same execution (atomic capture). Configs measured: loader filters + infinity settings.
testCard.expected: Exact entity+item parity left vs right; 578 items stationary; loader/chest configs survive copy and transfer.
testCard.forbidden: Do NOT freeze this pad (it is the steady-state class exemplar). Do NOT rebuild it from a script - the state is the artifact.
